title: The Washing of Hands
subtitle: The Pharisees come down from Jerusalem to accuse the disciples, and the Lord lays bare their traditions against the commandment of God

stillness: Be still, and turn from the outer to the inner.
Those who long to share the Bread that comes down from heaven
must cleanse their works again and again — with alms, with tears, and with the other fruits of righteousness.
Quiet now the mouth of your body, and open the mouth of your heart.
gospel: The scribes and Pharisees came from Jerusalem to him, saying:
"Why do your disciples transgress the tradition of the elders?
For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread."
But he answering said to them:
"Why do you also transgress the commandment of God for your tradition?
For God said: Honour your father and mother;
and: He that shall curse father or mother, let him die the death.
But you say: Whoever shall say to father or mother,
'Whatever gift is from me shall profit you' —
and he shall not honour his father or his mother.
And you have made void the commandment of God for your tradition.
This people honours me with their lips,
but their heart is far from me."
behold: See how great a malice appears in these men:
coming down from Jerusalem, the holy city, leaving the temple, they follow after him.
They were the elders of the people and the doctors of the Law,
and they came not to learn, but to accuse the Lord —
watching, lying in wait, that they might catch him in word or in deed.
See: they zealously guard the breaking of human traditions more than the divine commandments,
more than the Gospels, more than what is profitable.
Anxious much over the outer cleanness, and little over the inner,
they are the hypocrites and the pretenders —
reproaching others for small things, while they themselves are stained with grave ones,
seeing the speck in another's eye, and not the beam in their own.
Like to these are the many who wish to have a fair outside,
while within they are foul and rotten.
consider: The Lord did not condemn cleanness of the body;
this he condemned: that, leaving the inner cleanness, they thought the outer alone enough.
And these greedy Pharisees, to serve their own avarice,
under the name of piety brought in impiety.
They taught the sons against the parents,
saying it was better to offer to God what was owed to father and mother.
So the needy parent was refused, and the offering of the sons,
under the pretext of religion, passed into the gain of the priests.
You forsake the commandment of piety for the greed of impiety.
For nothing pleases God, whatever you offer him, if you neglect what you are bound to give.
A father is bound of himself to provide for his children, for he is the cause of their being;
but the son, too, is bound to provide his parents what they need, if they want and he is able.
Of what belongs to another, no one can make sacrifice to God.
Take heed, then, that you do not abuse this — as many do —
robbing your parents under the show of devotion.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, grant me so to keep the commandments of God without transgression
that I may set them before all things, one and all,
and depart from them on no occasion whatever.
Grant me also, in all that enters the mouth of my body, to resist gluttony,
and to strive above all after the inner cleanness of the heart,
and to guard diligently the mouth of my heart and of my body.
And because human watchfulness does not suffice unless the divine be present,
therefore, Lord, I humbly beg you:
set a guard over both these mouths of mine,
that nothing may enter or come forth from them
which can stain me spiritually in my soul, and before you.

latinJewel.latin: Necessarium est eos qui panem de cœlo descendentem participare desiderant, crebro eleemosynis, lacrymis, et aliis justitiæ fructibus sua opera purgare.
latinJewel.english: It is needful that those who desire to share the Bread that comes down from heaven should cleanse their works again and again — with alms, with tears, and with the other fruits of righteousness.
